question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that prune() deletes ContractResult rows at timestamps that may coincide with the FIRST_NONZERO_REWARD_RATE_TIMESTAMP used in RecalculatePendingRewardMigration, so that pruning removes contract execution records from before the first non-zero reward rate, causing the migration to compute incorrect pending rewards for contracts that were staking before that timestamp?
